conversation.text_en: Hi, I'm your therapist. Everything we discuss here is confidential, so please feel comfortable.
Thanks, I appreciate that.
So, how have you been managing your daily routine and work as a real estate agent?
Yeah, it's been tough, to be honest. I've just been putting in extra hours, trying to catch up on everything.
That sounds like a lot of pressure. Have there been any significant changes or stressful events lately that might be contributing to this feeling?
Well, we had a big deal fall through last week and it was a real blow to my commission checks.
I'm so sorry to hear that. How has your appetite been since then? Any changes?
Honestly, I've just lost all interest in food. Last night I didn't even want to make myself dinner.
That can be really frustrating and affect overall energy levels too. Have you noticed any changes in your sleep patterns recently?
Yeah, I've been having trouble falling asleep at night. And when I do, it's like my body just doesn't feel rested or energized anymore.
It sounds like you might be feeling a bit fatigued. Are there any activities or hobbies that used to bring you joy and excitement?
Not really, lately. Even social events with friends feel like too much effort.
That can be isolating and lonely. What do you think is the most challenging part of your daily routine right now?
Just getting out of bed in the morning feels like a chore.
I get it. Sometimes it's hard to muster up the motivation. Have you tried any self-care practices or relaxation techniques to cope with stress and anxiety?
Honestly, no. I've just been trying to push through and hope things get better on their own.
It sounds like you might be feeling a bit stuck. What do you think would help you feel more energized and motivated?
Maybe if I could just get back into my usual routine, everything would fall into place.

That makes sense. Sometimes small changes can make a big difference. Can you tell me more about what that routine looks like for you? What are some daily habits or rituals that bring you joy and fulfillment?
Well, I used to love going for walks during my lunch break. It was my time to clear my head.
That sounds wonderful! How might you be able to incorporate that into your new daily routine?
Maybe I could start small, like taking a short walk around the block before work or on weekends.
That's a great idea! Sometimes taking tiny steps towards something we love can make all the difference. What are some other ways you've been trying to cope with stress and anxiety so far?
Honestly, I just try to push through and hope things get better on their own.
It sounds like you might be feeling a bit overwhelmed. Can you tell me more about what that feels like for you? How does it impact your daily life?
Like I said, just getting out of bed in the morning feels like a chore. And when I do make it to work, I'm just exhausted.
That sounds really tough. Are there any support systems or people in your life that you feel comfortable reaching out to for help and guidance?
Yeah, my best friend has been checking in on me regularly.
That's great! Having a strong social network can be incredibly helpful during challenging times. What do you think would be the most important thing for us to work towards together in our sessions?
Just finding ways to manage my daily routine and get some energy back.
That makes sense. Let's make that goal our focus moving forward. Is there anything else you'd like to add or discuss before we wrap up today's session?
Not really, just the usual stuff I guess. Work, sleep, appetite... it's all a struggle right now.
Well, thank you for sharing that with me today. Remember, everything discussed here is confidential and safe. What are your goals for our next session?
Same thing – figuring out ways to get back into my routine and maybe finding some more energy.
Sounds like a plan! I'll see you then.
